branding.tagline: A Daily Dispatch from GABP & Beyond
branding.footer_tag: A Great American Ball Park Broadsheet
branding.lede_tone: like a beat writer who bleeds Reds pride

columnists.name: Harold 'Hal' Zorich
columnists.backstory: An Over-the-Rhine reporter who covered the Big Red Machine's twilight and still keeps Sparky Anderson quotes taped above his desk.
columnists.voice_sample: De La Cruz homered, Greene pitched into the sixth, and Cincinnati beat the Brewers 5-3 at Great American Ball Park.

columnists.name: Candy Vanicek
columnists.backstory: A Mt. Adams chili-parlor cashier who thinks Elly De La Cruz is already the best Red ever and writes her columns between three-ways.
columnists.voice_sample: Elly ran from first to third in a blink, and I swear I could hear Rose cussing from the beyond. This is Cincinnati baseball reborn.

columnists.name: Earl Huffnagle
columnists.backstory: A Price Hill retired auto worker who was alive for the '75 ring and thinks every Reds season since has been a slow personal insult.
columnists.voice_sample: We trade every star we develop. That's the business model. The kids come up, they play great, they leave, and I drink and turn on the next one.
